text.title: Design Patterns Overview Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: ADR proposes Strategy for a function with one implementation 'in case we need more'.
antipattern.fix: Reject; reintroduce when a second variant is documented.

antipattern.symptom: Logging + caching + retry + auth wrapped in 4-deep decorator stack; debugging requires unwrapping each layer.
antipattern.root-cause: r2 cost not assessed; decorator used as universal hammer.
antipattern.fix: Flatten into middleware composition with named layers; cap decorator depth at 2.

antipattern.symptom: Saga introduced for a transaction that already runs inside one DB.
antipattern.root-cause: r3 violated — cloud-native pattern without distributed need.
antipattern.fix: Collapse to local transaction; reserve sagas for genuine cross-service flows.

antipattern.symptom: ADR proposes Observer with no mention of Mediator or pub-sub queue.
antipattern.fix: Block ADR; require at least two alternatives.
